quick_facts.label: What it is
quick_facts.value: AFCAT (Air Force Common Admission Test) is the Indian Air Force’s online officer-entry examination for Flying and Ground Duty (Technical and Non-Technical) branches.

quick_facts.label: Recruiting authority
quick_facts.value: Indian Air Force (IAF), Government of India.

quick_facts.label: Latest notified cycle
quick_facts.value: AFCAT 02/2026 was notified for courses commencing in July 2027. Its application window was 20 May 2026, 11:00 hours to 19 June 2026, 23:30 hours; this deadline is past as of 18 July 2026.

quick_facts.label: AFCAT 02/2026 test date
quick_facts.value: The notified AFCAT 02/2026 online test date is 8 August 2026.

quick_facts.label: Examination structure
quick_facts.value: For AFCAT 02/2026, the online objective test is in English: 100 questions, 300 marks, and two hours. Correct answers receive three marks, incorrect answers lose one mark, and unattempted questions receive zero.

quick_facts.label: Selection after the test
quick_facts.value: Candidates who progress through the selection process undergo AFSB testing; candidates recommended by the board undergo medical examination. The IAF prepares an all-India merit list using written-test and AFSB-interview performance, subject to medical fitness and vacancies.

sections.heading: Purpose and officer-entry routes
paragraphs: AFCAT is an Indian Air Force officer-recruitment route, not an academic entrance examination. The AFCAT 02/2026 notification invites Indian citizens, men and women, for appointment as Group ‘A’ Gazetted Officers in Flying and Ground Duty—Technical and Non-Technical—branches, for courses commencing in July 2027.

The branch and commission options are cycle-specific. For AFCAT 02/2026, the notification lists Flying, Ground Duty (Technical), and Ground Duty (Non-Technical) vacancies; it also separately provides NCC Special Entry and GATE Score Entry routes. The notification expressly says vacancies are tentative and may change according to organisational requirements and training capacity.
bullets: Treat the current cycle notification as controlling: eligibility, vacancies, branches, medical standards, dates, and application requirements may be revised in later cycles.
For the technical branch, the 02/2026 notification also permits a GATE Score Entry route; shortlisted candidates under that route are exempt from AFCAT and are called directly for AFSB testing.

sections.heading: Eligibility: check branch-specific requirements
paragraphs: For AFCAT 02/2026, applicants must be Indian citizens. They must be unmarried when the course starts, and marriage is not permitted during training. The age reference date is 1 July 2027: Flying Branch applicants must be 20–24, with an upper limit of 26 for holders of a valid current DGCA-issued Commercial Pilot Licence; Ground Duty Technical and Non-Technical applicants must be 20–26.

Academic eligibility varies materially by branch. The IAF’s AFCAT entry guidance states that Flying and Weapon Systems candidates need 10+2 Mathematics and Physics with at least 50% in each, plus an eligible graduation/engineering qualification generally with at least 60%; Ground Duty Technical requires 10+2 Physics and Mathematics plus a qualifying engineering/technology or listed equivalent qualification; Accounts, Meteorology, Education, Administration, and Logistics each have their own prescribed degree pathways.

Final-year or final-semester students may apply under the conditions in the notification, including no current backlog and submission of required proof by the stipulated date. Medical fitness is compulsory, with more stringent standards for aircrew; candidates should review the medical appendix before selecting a branch.
bullets: Do not assume that every bachelor’s degree qualifies for every AFCAT branch. Match your exact subjects, degree title, marks, 10+2 subjects, age, marital status, and medical eligibility to the current notification.

For AFCAT 02/2026, the notification specifies exact dates of birth for the July 2027 course: 2 July 2003–1 July 2007 for standard Flying Branch eligibility, and 2 July 2001–1 July 2007 for Ground Duty eligibility, inclusive.

sections.heading: Exam format and syllabus
paragraphs: In AFCAT 02/2026, AFCAT is an English-language, computer-based objective examination. It has 100 questions for 300 marks in two hours, covering General Awareness, Verbal Ability in English, Numerical Ability, and Reasoning and Military Aptitude. The notified marking scheme is +3 for a correct answer, −1 for an incorrect answer, and 0 for an unattempted question.

The notified syllabus includes English comprehension, grammar, vocabulary and sentence work; General Awareness spanning history, geography, polity, economy, basic science, technology, current affairs and defence; matriculation-level Numerical Ability; and verbal/non-verbal reasoning. The notification states that Numerical Ability is at matriculation level, while other subjects are at Indian-university graduation level.
If an AFCAT cycle uses more than one shift, the notification provides for normalisation of marks before results. The IAF may set qualifying marks.
bullets: Use the official cycle notification rather than coaching-material patterns, because format and operational details can change.
The IAF makes an online practice test available through its AFCAT portal, according to the 02/2026 notification.

sections.heading: Application process and current-cycle status
paragraphs: Applicants apply online through the AFCAT portal identified in the notification. For AFCAT 02/2026, online registration required Aadhaar for registration, a valid email address and mobile number, a recent photograph, signature, thumb impression, and accurate academic and personal information. The notification warns that incorrect information can lead to cancellation of candidature and that details generally cannot be changed after final submission.

For the AFCAT 02/2026 cycle, the published application period ran from 20 May 2026 at 11:00 hours to 19 June 2026 at 23:30 hours, so it is closed as of 18 July 2026. The published AFCAT-entry examination fee for that cycle was ₹550 plus GST, non-refundable; the notification states that this fee did not apply to NCC Special Entry or GATE Score Entry. Fees and future deadlines are variable and must be checked in the next official notification.

After a successful online application, eligible candidates receive an e-admit card; for AFCAT 02/2026, the notification said it would be issued nine days before the examination and downloadable from the AFCAT portal. No admit card would be posted.
bullets: Use only the official portal and retain the application/transaction details.
Government employees must obtain the required departmental permission and produce an NOC at AFSB testing, where applicable.
Read the current notification before submitting: the IAF says the notification is guidance subject to prevailing IAF/Government policy and may change without notice.

sections.heading: Preparation priorities
paragraphs: Build preparation around the official syllabus and timed online practice. For the AFCAT written test, prioritise English, General Awareness—including current national/international affairs and defence—matriculation-level arithmetic, and verbal/non-verbal reasoning. Practise under the notified two-hour, 100-question format and account for the −1 negative mark when deciding whether to guess.

Written-test preparation alone is insufficient for the officer-selection pathway. Candidates who clear the relevant stage are assessed at an Air Force Selection Board through screening, psychological tests, group tests, and an interview; Stage II normally runs over the next three to four days after Stage I qualification. Medical fitness is also required.

Before applying, review branch medical standards and documentation requirements early—especially for Flying Branch candidates, whose vision, anthropometry, and other standards are more stringent. This is a suitability and eligibility check, not something to defer until after the written test.
bullets: Use the IAF AFCAT portal’s official practice test in addition to revision and mock tests.
Develop clear communication, group-task participation, officer-like judgement, and physical readiness for AFSB rather than treating it as a purely written-exam process.
Avoid relying on an old notification for dates, degree lists, documents, or medical standards.

sections.heading: What qualifying AFCAT can lead to
paragraphs: AFCAT marks can lead to progression to AFSB testing; they do not by themselves secure appointment. Candidates recommended by AFSB proceed to medical examination, and final joining depends on medical fitness, merit position, vacancies, and the IAF’s final selection process.

Successful candidates join the relevant IAF training establishment according to their allocated branch and course. The official IAF entry page identifies the Air Force Academy for Flying and Ground Duty training, with Ground Duty Technical trainees also proceeding to the Air Force Technical College.

Commission terms differ by branch and cycle. In the 02/2026 notification, the Flying Branch AFCAT route is listed under Short Service Commission; Ground Duty technical and non-technical entries include commission options stated in the vacancy table and commission provisions. Candidates should assess the specific notification rather than assume a permanent commission is available for a chosen branch.
bullets: Final branch allocation is subject to eligibility, suitability, vacancy availability, and IAF policy; an applicant’s preferences are not a guarantee of allocation.
The all-India merit list is prepared based on written-test and AFSB-interview performance, subject to medical fitness and vacancies.
